stimulus.title: Version Tombstone failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Overwriting latest records is enough for audits and corrections.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain version lineage, supersession, disappearance, deletion, restoration, and report impact under retention policy.

options.text: Run the Version Tombstone boundary case for multi-signal content identity and change ledger, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Version Tombstone result because visible multi-signal content identity and change ledger output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Version Tombstone.

For diagnose the hidden failure, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Version Tombstone for multi-signal content identity and change ledger.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Version Tombstone
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Version Tombstone: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 7793 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate stage digests, exact identity, near-duplicate review, change classes, versions, and tombstones; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain version lineage, supersession, disappearance, deletion, restoration, and report impact under retention policy.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Version Tombstone.
steps.why: Retrieving Version Tombstone through the concrete multi-signal content identity and change ledger case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Version Tombstone, keep Version Tombstone connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain version lineage, supersession, disappearance, deletion, restoration, and report impact under retention policy.
